reviews.author: Olivier D.
reviews.text: Order the croque-monsieur and a citron pressé. The combo is perfect.

reviews.author: Antoine B.
reviews.text: Expensive, yes. But you are not paying for coffee, you are paying for the room you are sitting in.

reviews.author: Tom H.
reviews.text: Expensive, yes. But you are not paying for coffee, you are paying for the room you are sitting in.

reviews.author: Luis G.
reviews.text: Waiter was grumpy but it somehow felt appropriate.

reviews.author: Hugo L.
reviews.text: If you go, sit facing the street. Trust me.

reviews.author: Thomas W.
reviews.text: The terrasse is everything you imagine. Hot chocolate thick enough to stand a spoon in.

reviews.author: Pierre L.
reviews.text: I enjoyed it but I can think of 10 better places in the 6th.

reviews.author: Yuki S.
reviews.text: The history is palpable. Sartre, Beauvoir, Picasso. They all sat at these tables.

reviews.author: Antoine B.
reviews.text: Came on a Tuesday at 3pm. Got the corner table. Did not move for three hours. No regrets.

reviews.author: Mia C.
reviews.text: Croque-monsieur is the platonic ideal of a croque-monsieur.

reviews.author: Lila N.
reviews.text: Expensive, yes. But you are not paying for coffee, you are paying for the room you are sitting in.

reviews.author: Ben R.
reviews.text: Waiter was grumpy but it somehow felt appropriate.

reviews.author: Tom H.
reviews.text: Best people-watching spot in Saint-Germain. Also the worst if you are easily distracted.

reviews.author: Anna S.
reviews.text: Service was cold. Waiter looked through me like I was not there.

reviews.author: Sven O.
reviews.text: We came back twice in four days. That says everything.

reviews.author: Yuki S.
reviews.text: Expensive, yes. But you are not paying for coffee, you are paying for the room you are sitting in.

reviews.text: Order the croque-monsieur and a citron pressé. The combo is perfect.

reviews.author: Mia C.
reviews.text: Worth one visit for the cultural résumé alone.

reviews.author: Yuki S.
reviews.text: Waiter was grumpy but it somehow felt appropriate.

reviews.author: Marco R.
reviews.text: Hot chocolate is the most over-the-top thing I have ever drunk. Worth every euro.

reviews.author: Hannah K.
reviews.text: Watched an old man read Le Monde for two hours next to me. Did not look up once. Aspirational.

reviews.author: Aaliyah K.
reviews.text: Came on a Tuesday at 3pm. Got the corner table. Did not move for three hours. No regrets.

reviews.author: Marie C.
reviews.text: Came in the rain, sat inside. Felt like being in a novel. Worth the price once.

reviews.author: Tom H.
reviews.text: Hot chocolate is the most over-the-top thing I have ever drunk. Worth every euro.

reviews.author: Mia C.
reviews.text: Expensive, yes. But you are not paying for coffee, you are paying for the room you are sitting in.

reviews.author: Nora E.
reviews.text: Tarte tatin is excellent. The whipped crème fraîche is the move.

reviews.author: Inès D.
reviews.text: The history is palpable. Sartre, Beauvoir, Picasso. They all sat at these tables.

reviews.author: Inès D.
reviews.text: The terrace in winter with blankets is something else. Pure Parisian cliché, but you have to do it.

reviews.author: Aaliyah K.

reviews.author: Luis G.
reviews.text: It is what it is. Famous café, famous prices, decent coffee.

reviews.author: Pierre L.
reviews.text: Tarte tatin is excellent. The whipped crème fraîche is the move.

reviews.author: Nora E.
reviews.text: If you want the experience, go. If you want good coffee, go literally anywhere else.
